data_version.rule: Versioned D1 dataset identifier; any source mapping or lifecycle semantic change requires a new data_version before formal use.

universe_id.rule: Research universe identifier; D1-T01 does not materialize membership rows and does not expand the G0 universe.

time_segment_id.rule: Configured time segment key; security identity rows must not infer facts outside the approved segment boundary.

security_id.rule: Project-internal stable key generated as CN.{exchange}.{ticker}; it is not a supplier code and not a raw source symbol.

ticker.rule: Six digit A-share ticker normalized without exchange suffix or vendor prefix; leading zeros must be preserved.

exchange.rule: Canonical exchange code from the project enum: SSE or SZSE.

security_name.rule: Issuer/security display name as observed in the approved source snapshot; name changes require snapshot provenance.

listing_date.rule: First exchange listing date for the security identity in Asia/Shanghai exchange-date semantics.

delisting_date.rule: Final delisting date when explicitly available from source evidence; null means no observed delisting fact, not false.

security_type.rule: Canonical project security type; D1-T01 permits only A_SHARE_COMMON for this contract version.

source_registry_id.rule: Registry id of the approved source snapshot used for the row; formal ingestion remains unauthorized in D1-T01.

source_snapshot_id.rule: Immutable raw snapshot reference required before any future loader can write formal rows.

run_id.rule: Run manifest id required before formal materialization; D1-T01 does not create run manifests.

identifier_policy.security_id_role: security_id is the project's stable internal identity key for joins; downstream research must use it instead of vendor symbols.
identifier_policy.not_equal_to_vendor_code_rule: Vendor codes such as 600000.SH, SH600000, 000001.SZ, or source-specific symbols must be retained as source symbols and normalized into security_id only through reviewed mapping rules.
identifier_policy.stability_rule: For the same listed security identity, security_id remains stable across source snapshots unless the exchange or ticker identity itself changes; corrections require new snapshot provenance and review.

exchange_policy.exchange_code_system: Use project canonical exchange codes: SSE for Shanghai Stock Exchange and SZSE for Shenzhen Stock Exchange.

ticker_policy.normalization_rule: Strip vendor exchange suffixes/prefixes only after source_registry_id and source_symbol have been retained in raw snapshot evidence; output ticker must be exactly six ASCII digits.
ticker_policy.leading_zero_rule: Treat ticker as text; never parse as integer or drop leading zeros.

security_type_policy.type_code_system: Use project canonical security type codes. ST status is a trading/status fact and must not be encoded as security_type.

source_symbol_policy.traceability_rule: Every future source symbol mapping must be traceable to source_registry_id, source_snapshot_id, original source_symbol, observed_at or retrieval time, and the normalization rule version.
source_symbol_policy.normalization_boundary: D1-T01 defines mapping rules only; it does not add a source_symbol column to d1.security_master and does not store vendor payloads in DuckDB.
prohibited_assumptions: Do not assume a supplier code is equal to security_id.
Do not infer exchange solely from ticker prefix without source evidence.
Do not treat wrapper-returned symbols as publisher identity.
Do not convert unknown mapping quality to false.

lifecycle_policy.listing_date_rule: listing_date is the exchange listing date observed from approved source evidence and must be <= delisting_date when delisting_date is present.
lifecycle_policy.delisting_date_rule: delisting_date may be null only when the source snapshot lacks an explicit delisting fact; null must not be interpreted as active membership or tradability.
lifecycle_policy.revision_rule: Any correction to listing_date, delisting_date, ticker, exchange, security_name, or security_type requires new source_snapshot_id or data_version and must not rewrite old evidence.

source_registry_requirements.reason: HiThink remains only a candidate source for security_master; source terms, as-of semantics, revision behavior, raw snapshot retention, manifests, and run authorization are not closed.

future_loader_requirements: Future loader must read only approved raw snapshots and manifests, never live APIs.
Future loader must preserve raw source_symbol provenance outside DuckDB raw byte storage.

Future loader must fail non-zero on duplicate primary keys, duplicate ticker/exchange mappings, invalid security_id format, invalid exchange, invalid security_type, date-order violations, or missing source snapshot references.
Future loader must not populate d1.security_master until source terms, raw snapshot, manifest, as-of, revision, and run authorization gates pass.
